sae_features.label: UBC/T-box and acidic low-complexity
sae_features.description: Domain-scale recognition of soluble eukaryotic interaction modules, most prominently the ubiquitin‑conjugating UBC/UEV fold and the T‑box DNA‑binding domain, with secondary activation of other cytosolic regulatory domains (e.g., inward‑rectifier K+ channel cytoplasmic tails) and, in proteins lacking these hallmark modules, extended low‑complexity acidic or Gly/Ser/Pro‑rich regions in soluble nuclear/cytosolic regulators. Activation is broad across eukaryotes (including large DNA viruses) and occasionally touches ubiquitin‑pathway components beyond E2s.

sae_features.label: C-terminal low-complexity IDRs
sae_features.description: A cross-kingdom feature that marks long, compositionally biased intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs), especially P/S/T/G- and repeat-rich segments (poly-Pro, Gly runs, Q/H-rich) that typically occur in mid-to-C-terminal tails of transcription factors and in cytoplasmic tails of single-pass membrane receptors; it ignores folded domains.

sae_features.label: N-terminal leader/N-cap detector
sae_features.description: Detector of N‑terminal leader/pro‑regions and domain N‑caps: long, low‑complexity, often basic and disordered stretches at the protein’s start (or at the start of the first domain) that precede and cap the stable globular core; includes signal peptides/propeptides in secreted precursors, flexible N‑terminal tails of viral structural proteins, and short disordered N‑extensions that bleed into the first helix/strand of the initial domain

sae_features.label: Intrinsic disorder with aromatic hotspots
sae_features.description: Intrinsic-disorder/low‑complexity recognition with aromatic hotspots: the feature preferentially activates on long, compositionally biased, low‑pLDDT coils/tails (often Pro/Ser/Gly‑rich) that flank structured domains, and in proteins dominated by folded domains it collapses to sharp peaks on isolated Trp/Tyr/His residues (e.g., aromatic anchors or active‑site ligands), while remaining suppressed over well‑packed domain cores.

sae_features.label: Conserved structured regulatory domains
sae_features.description: Conserved, structured regulatory domains from eukaryotic proteins—catalytic cores and nucleic‑acid/partner‑binding modules used in signaling and gene‑expression control (e.g., SET methyltransferase, FCP1/CTD Ser/Thr phosphatase, protein tyrosine phosphatase, OTU deubiquitinase, T‑box DNA‑binding, YTH m6A reader, DUF1669/CK1‑interacting, and septin GTPase domains).

sae_features.label: Disordered TF activation tails
sae_features.description: Intrinsically disordered, low‑complexity transactivation regions of metazoan transcription factors—Ser/Pro/Gln/Thr/Gly‑rich IDRs with short repeats and PTM hotspots—typically located in N‑ or C‑terminal regulatory tails and excluded from the folded DNA‑binding domain.

sae_features.label: Polar beta-sheet ligand-binding surfaces
sae_features.description: Extended, polar beta-sheet/loop recognition surfaces of beta‑rich domains that mediate binding to diverse ligands (DNA, RNA, carbohydrates, and metals). The feature favors antiparallel β‑strand cores plus adjacent turns enriched in small/polar residues (G, S, T, D, N) with Gly/Pro turns, and often includes His/Cys when metal coordination is involved. It is common in secreted/periplasmic or plastid‑targeted modules as well as nuclear DNA‑binding domains (e.g., T‑box).

sae_features.label: TF C-terminal transactivation-like IDRs
sae_features.description: Eukaryotic transcription-regulatory low-complexity IDRs, typically the C-terminal transactivation-like tails of transcription factors, enriched in Pro/Ser/Thr/Gln/Gly (proline-rich repeats, GP/PG runs, and SP/TP dipeptides) and positioned just outside structured DNA-binding domains; similar compositionally biased regulatory IDRs in other nuclear RNA-binding regulators can also trigger the feature.
